events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We need answer as raw JSON only. Need interpret scenario. Image shows micron gauge readings at isolation t=0: 397 microns. At t=10 min: 1318 microns. Standing decay test with pump isolated. Decay from 397 to 1318 in 10 min is significant rise. Criteria for acceptable standing vacuum test: typically less than 500 microns and not rising above 500 microns (or rise less than 500 microns?) Industry standard: evacuate to below 500 microns, isolate pump, vacuum should not rise more than 500 microns over 10 minutes (or hold below 500-1000 depending). Many HVAC: system should hold 500 microns or lower, with no rise above 500 microns after 10 min. Some specify decay < 500 microns over 10 min. Here initial 397 (pass level), final 1318 (high) indicating leak(s), outgassing, or moisture. 1318 > 1000, definitely fail. Rate = (1318-397)/10 = 92.1 microns/min. Value should be 10-minute reading 1318. Decision fail. Risk: high? Non-compliant, high because moisture/air contamination can cause acid formation, compressor damage, etc. Work_stage tested/inspected. Component_conditions non-compliant maybe installed-defective due leak. percent_complete maybe 70. Findings: vacuum decay excessive; system fails standing vacuum test; likely leak or moisture. Actions: re-evacuate, leak test with nitrogen, repair leaks/braze, replace filter drier, re-evacuate and retest per OEM. Rationale: rise from 397 to 1318 microns in 10 min exceeds allowable vacuum hold; indicates leak, contamination, or outgassing; system not ready for refrigerant charge. References: EPA 608 (proper evacuation requirements), manufacturer specs. Need output exactly JSON shape. Use order/workable/sound_source/event_time default. event_time 0? or leave 0. order empty list? The schema uses order array. Not audio, so empty list. workable empty list. sound_source "" perhaps. value 1318. rate 92.1. confidence 0.95.

Need raw JSON no markdown.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "tested/inspected",
  "component_conditions": ["non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 70,
  "value": 1318,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0,
  "rate": 92.1,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Standing vacuum decay test failed: pressure rose from 397 microns to 1318 microns in 10 minutes after pump isolation",
    "Final vacuum level exceeds acceptable limits for refrigerant charging",
    "Indicates likely leak, moisture, or outgassing in the system"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Do not charge refrigerant",
    "Pressurize system with dry nitrogen and perform leak detection",
    "Repair any leaks and re-braze as needed",
    "Replace liquid-line filter drier and deep evacuate again",
    "Repeat standing decay test until it holds within manufacturer/OEM limits"
  ],
  "rationale": "The vacuum rose from 397 to 1318 microns in 10 minutes, a 92 micron/min decay. This exceeds typical OEM/EPA-acceptable vacuum hold criteria and indicates the system will not maintain a deep vacuum, risking contamination, moisture, and compressor damage if charged.",
  "references": [
    "EPA 608 - evacuation requirements",
    "OEM service specifications for standing vacuum/decay test"
  ]
}
